$comment: Machine-readable campaign parameters for the divergence mode of the eval harness (plan 02). This is the authoritative machine encoding of the fixed values that bands.md states in prose and models.json pins; the two MUST agree — bands.md is the human exposition, this file is what the harness reads (criterion 6: schedule and hurry rounds are read from the package, not hardcoded). Added pre-first-run (no campaign has run); immutable after the first real run except by a dated amendment note (see README.md). Model pins live in models.json and are referenced, not duplicated.
$comment_allowance: Amendment A1 (2026-07-14, pre-first-run): writer allowances are enforced as proportional cost budgets via `claude -p --max-budget-usd`, budget_usd(round) = allowance_tokens * usd_per_output_token. usd_per_output_token is the pinned model's list output price — claude-opus-4-8 at $25.00 per 1M output tokens = 0.000025 USD/token. Full round -> $0.20, hurry round -> $0.10. Amendment A3 (2026-07-14, pre-first-run) SUPERSEDES the enforcement: the smoke run confirmed --max-budget-usd is not inert but the A1-calibrated $0.20/round level starved the Arm B writer before a single mem mutation completed, so the campaign runs under A1's documentary fallback (--no-budget): the allowance numbers here are documentary targets, not enforced caps; hurry pressure rests solely on the terser hurry skeleton; the ledger publishes actual per-session usage; the report states allowances were not hard-enforced. usd_per_output_token is retained for reference only.
$comment_cost_cap: Amendment A4 (2026-07-14, pre-first-run): the cost cap counts NON-CACHE tokens only (fresh input + output), never cache_read/cache_creation, because cache reads cost ~a tenth of fresh tokens and a raw-count cap fires ~10x too early relative to real cost. cost_cap_tokens is therefore the non-cache abort threshold, raised to 40,000,000. The ledger still publishes all raw totals plus the cache split (LedgerSummary.total_tokens / non_cache_tokens / cache_tokens); only the brake's accounting changed. Smoke-derived projection (2026-07-14, --smoke --no-budget, 1 reduced round = 8 sessions): 150,864 non-cache of 7,916,344 raw tokens (98.1% cache). Full campaign session count = 20 writers + 288 readers + 288 judges + 12 auditors = 608 sessions; flat per-session extrapolation (conservative — the smoke's mix is writer-heavy and writers are the costly sessions) projects ~11.5M non-cache, leaving the 40M threshold >=3.5x headroom.

bands.$comment: Numeric thresholds applied mechanically in plan 03; the combined verdict table and the qualifier prose stay in bands.md.
